release_review.summary: Dependency security refresh updates Flowersec and browser UI packages without changing the Runtime Service protocol window.
checked_surfaces: Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1; no runtime-control or desktop bridge payload fields changed in this dependency refresh
Flowersec Go and TypeScript dependencies are consumed from published v0.19.10 artifacts only, with no local sibling repository wiring or go.work involvement
Floe Webapp packages are consumed from published v0.36.68 artifacts only; Env App protocol and boot helpers keep the same public contract shape
Desktop and Env App package locks refresh DOMPurify and Undici patched transitive versions while preserving existing startup, attach, and Local UI health flows
Runtime binary release artifacts keep the same command surface and embedded asset build path; the release updates dependency provenance rather than Runtime Service wire semantics
Compatibility epoch, minimum Desktop version, and minimum Runtime version remain unchanged because no Runtime Service protocol fields, control methods, or minimum client requirements changed
release_review.same_window_rationale: This release is a dependency security refresh. The Runtime Service protocol remains redeven-runtime-v1 with compatibility_epoch 5, minimum_desktop_version v0.7.0, and minimum_runtime_version v0.7.0. Flowersec, Floe Webapp, DOMPurify, and Undici updates change dependency provenance and patched package contents, not Runtime Service request/response schemas, desktop bridge behavior, startup report format, sys.ping semantics, Env App maintenance UI protocol, or SSH runtime bootstrap compatibility.
